summary.scholar: The personal computer revolution began in the mid-1970s with the Altair 8800 (1975) and exploded with the Apple II (1977) and IBM PC (1981). Steve Jobs and Steve Wozniak founded Apple Computer in a garage, while Bill Gates and Paul Allen created Microsoft. PCs transformed business, education, and daily life, putting computing power that once filled entire rooms onto every desk.
summary.explorer: In the 1970s, computers went from filling whole rooms to sitting on your desk! Steve Jobs and Steve Wozniak started Apple in a garage, and Bill Gates started Microsoft. These personal computers changed everything — how people work, learn, play games, and even how they talk to each other.

text.scholar: Apple Computer was founded in Steve Jobs's parents' garage in Los Altos, California in 1976. The company would become the most valuable in the world.
text.explorer: Apple started in a GARAGE! Steve Jobs and his friend Steve Wozniak built their first computers there. Now Apple is worth trillions of dollars.

sources.citation: Miller Center: U.S. Presidents and American History
